name: Hungarian-Style Pork Chops and Potatoes
recipeIngredient: 1/8 teaspoon Fresh ground pepper, to taste
1/4 cup Sour cream, or to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle both sides of the pork chops lightly with salt and pepper.
2. Heat a large heavy skillet; add the pork chops and brown quickly on both sides. Remove and set aside.
3. Add butter to the drippings and sauté onion until tender, stirring occasionally.
4. Stir in 1 cup broth, paprika, and 1/4 teaspoon salt. Add potatoes.
5. Bring to a boil, cover, and cook over moderate heat about 10 minutes, shaking the pan occasionally.
6. Add the pork chops. Cook an additional 10 to 15 minutes, or until the potatoes and chops are tender, turning occasionally and adding the remaining 1/2 cup broth if needed for moisture.
7. Remove potatoes and pork chops to a heated platter.
8. Stir sour cream into the pan juices.
9. Heat through but do not boil; pour over the potatoes and chops.
10. Season with salt and pepper to taste. If desired, sprinkle with minced parsley.
